meta.description: Known scam, phishing, and fraud domains compiled from community reports, FTC filings, SEC alerts, EFCC advisories, and scam-reporting platforms. Add new entries at the bottom — keep alphabetical order per category.
sources: FBI IC3 Internet Crime Complaint Center

domains.notes: Fake PancakeSwap airdrop claim. Connect wallet and drain all tokens. Uses .finance TLD to appear legitimate. Targets BNB Chain users.
Phishing clone of 1inch DEX aggregator. .win TLD variant used for typosquatting. Swaps intended transactions for malicious token approvals.
Reported by multiple victims. Blocks withdrawals above $500.
Phishing site claiming Aave v4 upgrade. Users must 'migrate' positions. Drains deposited assets and approves malicious token spend.
Fake Adobe Creative Cloud renewal page. Harvests payment details and account credentials. Targets creative professionals and businesses.
Claims AI algorithm generates 3% daily. No regulatory registration found.
AI Wealth Inc. WhatsApp investment club. Part of $14M SEC case. Ran Jan-June 2024. Promoted fake AI-generated investment tips and SCT security token.
Impersonated a registered NJ broker-dealer to scam investors seeking recovery. Claimed to be a recovery intelligence firm. Used phony testimonials and stock photos. Demanded crypto payments.
Phishing site impersonating Amazon. Targets users with fake refund notifications. Harvests credit card and banking credentials. High-volume distribution via email/SMS.
Apple ID phishing page. Claims account locked for verification. Harvests Apple ID credentials and payment information. Targets iOS users globally.
Fake Arbitrum ARB token distribution. Claims unclaimed tokens available. Drains all Arbitrum-connected wallets. Uses .io for tech legitimacy.
FCA-listed unlicensed firm. Domain registered 3 weeks before launch.
Phishing site impersonating Balancer DeFi protocol. Fake liquidity pool migration. Drains all token balances on connection.
Fake Base L2 rewards distribution. Claims early user rewards. Drains entire wallet on Base and Ethereum mainnet. Not affiliated with Coinbase.
Berge Blockchain Technology fake platform established June 2022. Part of $14M social media investment scam. Promoted via AI Wealth and Lane Wealth WhatsApp groups. Currently delinquent.
Promises 15% weekly ROI. Multiple withdrawal complaints filed.
Impersonates Binance exchange. Fake Elon Musk/crypto celebrity giveaway. Send crypto to receive double back — classic giveaway scam. Uses .cc TLD for evasion.
Binary options retail trading is banned in EU, UK, Australia and Canada.
UK FCA-listed unauthorised firm. Claims address at Tower Hill, London. No regulatory registration. Targeting UK investors with unlicensed crypto/financial services.
Classic doubler Ponzi. Pays early investors from new deposits until collapse.
Phishing clone of blockchain.com. Harvests seed phrases under guise of verification.
ASIC-listed unlicensed foreign exchange dealer.
Recovery scam using .io domain for tech credibility illusion. Promises crypto asset recovery. No regulatory oversight. Upfront fee model.
Money flip operation. Promises 4x return within 24 hours.
Impersonates Central Bank of Nigeria. Claims COVID-19 or business grants available. Harvests BVN, banking details, and personal data for identity theft.
Phishing clone of Chase Bank. Harvests online banking credentials and personal information. Part of large-scale banking phishing campaign.
Cirkor Inc. fake trading platform established May 2024. Part of $14M AIIEF/Zenith WhatsApp investment club scam. Administratively dissolved Oct 2025. Fake STO for HumanBlock crypto.
Third company in FBI 2024 seizure alongside MyChargeBack and Payback Ltd. Targeted crypto/forex victims through aggressive advertising and fake testimonials.
Spoofs Coinbase Pro security alerts to harvest login credentials.

First-ever SEC pig butchering case alongside NanoBit. Romance scam via LinkedIn/Instagram then WhatsApp. 11+ victims lost $2.2M+. Promised 3% daily returns from staking/mining. Blackmailed victims with romantic messages.
Fake COMP token distribution event. Claims retroactive rewards available. Drains entire wallet balance upon interaction. Not affiliated with Compound.
Targets previous crypto fraud victims. Charges upfront recovery fees then disappears.
Sells non-existent cloud mining contracts. No physical mining infrastructure.
Fake Curve Finance rewards claim. Targets Curve LP token holders. Drains entire wallet including staked positions. Not affiliated with Curve.
Unlicensed forex signal service. Sells VIP memberships with no verifiable track record.
Shipping reshipment scam targeting e-commerce users. Claims package delivery failed. Requests payment for reshipping and harvests payment details.
Shows manipulated dashboard profits. Withdrawals declined above $200.
Classic send-1-get-2 Ponzi. Pays a few early senders to manufacture trust.
Automated bot subscription that claims 5% daily. No verifiable bot exists.
EFCC officially warned: this domain is not affiliated with the EFCC. Collects data for identity theft.
Impersonates Elon Musk. Promises to double any Bitcoin sent to listed wallet addresses.
Fake Gulf-region job offers. Charges visa processing fees. No jobs exist.
Fake Ethena Labs airdrop claim. Promises ENA tokens. Drains wallet including sUSDe positions. Part of wave of Ethena-themed phishing.
Phishing site targeting Ethereum holders during protocol upgrades. Claims users must 'migrate' ETH. Connect wallet to drain funds. Not affiliated with Ethereum Foundation.
Pig-butchering hybrid. Victim lured via social media romance then directed to this platform.
Phishing site targeting Facebook business accounts. Claims account verification required. Hijacks business pages and ad accounts. Part of Meta's most-attacked brand.
Targets Nigerian users. Claims to flip ₦10,000 to ₦50,000 in 30 minutes.
Phishing site impersonating Fidelity Bank Nigeria. Harvests internet banking credentials.
Unlicensed forex investment scheme. Multiple FTC complaints on file.
Recovery scam targeting victims of previous frauds. Promises fund recovery through crypto tracing and legal escalation. No verifiable regulatory registration.
Charges ₦5,000 registration fee for non-existent freelance contracts.
Generic recovery scam. No verifiable licensing or track record. Charges upfront fees with no results. Part of broader recovery fraud ecosystem.
FSCA-listed unlicensed entity targeting Southern and West African investors.
Phishing page mimicking GTBank internet banking portal. Not affiliated with Guaranty Trust Bank.
Domain name itself is a red flag — no forex profit is ever guaranteed under any circumstances.
FCA-listed unauthorised entity. Claims Edinburgh, UK address at 61 Dublin Street. Name itself is a red flag — guarantees in trading are impossible. Email linked to genesismininginc.com.
Purported crypto trading platform in $14M investment confidence scam. Operated Jan 2024-Jan 2025 via WhatsApp investment clubs. Promoted fake Security Token Offerings. Delinquent status.
Sells fake 'hacking tools' or charges to hack social media accounts on behalf of victims.
Claims to be NZ-based trade/investment company. Uses stock photos as team members. Same template as Quantum TradexPro and Nova Fx Plus. Guaranteed ROI claims. Active fraudulent operation.
High Yield Investment Program. Pays early investors from later deposits in classic Ponzi structure.
Fictitious company promoted by AIIEF/Zenith groups. Fake HMB crypto token. Part of $14M fraud. No real company or product behind the offering.
Next-generation 419 scam. Claims large inheritance from distant relative. Requires 'legal fees' and 'processing charges' for fund release. Professional-looking documentation.
Promises instant Bitcoin credit on deposit. Withdrawals are blocked after initial small test payout.
FCA-listed unauthorized investment firm operating from an unverifiable offshore address.
Impersonates IRS during tax season. Claims direct tax refund available. Harvests SSN, banking details, and personal information. Not affiliated with the IRS.
Lane Wealth Inc. WhatsApp investment club. Ran Jan-June 2024. Promoted Morocoin and Berge fake platforms. AI-generated tips used to build trust.
Phishing site impersonating LinkedIn Premium billing. Harvests payment details and professional credentials. Targets business professionals.
Approves loans instantly then requests insurance fee payment upfront. No loan is ever disbursed.
Phishing clone of Luno exchange (different from lunoexchange.net in original list). Additional domain operated by same scam network. Captures login credentials and 2FA.
Phishing clone of Luno exchange. Not affiliated with Luno (luno.com). Captures login and 2FA tokens.
Fake MetaMask recovery portal. Sole purpose is to harvest wallet seed phrases.
Fake forex broker operating without any license. Office address is fabricated.
Impersonates Microsoft 365 security alerts. Harvests corporate login credentials. Targets business users with fake security notifications.
Bitcoin Ponzi with fake live withdrawal feeds displayed on homepage.
FBI seized domains in 2024. Parent company Cactil LLC. Charged significant upfront fees with no verifiable recovery outcomes. Targeted crypto/fx fraud victims.
Targets Nigerians. Claims to multiply naira sent via multiple social media recruitment channels.
First-ever SEC pig butchering case. Fake crypto platform that stole ~$968K from 18+ victims. Scammers impersonated financial professionals in WhatsApp groups. Funds wired to Hong Kong.
Phishing site impersonating Netflix. Claims billing information needs update. Harvests credit card details and account credentials. Mass-distributed via email.
FCA-listed unauthorised firm with fake New York address. Contact numbers +13322860609 and +13479835187. Not registered with any securities regulator.
Fake international scholarship portal. Charges application and document processing fees.
Impersonates Nigeria Customs Service. Claims to auction seized vehicles/goods at low prices. Advance fee fraud — victims pay 'processing fees' for non-existent items.
Classic 419 advance fee fraud. Victim promised large inheritance disbursement in exchange for small fee.
Impersonates NNPC (Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation). Offers fake oil supply contracts. Advance fee fraud targeting local and international businesses.
Active crypto wallet phishing operation. Uses pixel-perfect clones of legitimate sites. Malicious approval phishing to drain wallets. Pig butchering component.
Multi-asset fake platform. Domain registered July 2025. Claims regulation in SVG, Seychelles, South Africa — none verified. Same layout as Quantum TradexPro. Fake executive identities.
Not affiliated with OKX (okx.com). Pig butchering platform targeting OKX brand reputation.
Fake OpenSea free minting site. Claims exclusive NFT drop. Wallet drain via malicious contract approval. Targets NFT collectors and traders.
Fake Optimism OP airdrop claim page. Targets Ethereum L2 users. Drains tokens across connected chains upon wallet approval.
CFTC-registered complaint. Manipulates trade outcomes to ensure customer losses.
Israel-based recovery company. FBI seized 2024. Heavily marketed to US victims. Claimed $5M+ recovered in 2023. Upfront fees, unverifiable success claims.
Markets itself as recovery service. Claims no upfront fees but verification difficult. Part of an industry where 90%+ of operations are fraudulent.
Mass-distributed phishing email links. Not affiliated with PayPal Inc.
Fake Pendle Finance yield token claim. Targets Pendle YT token holders. Malicious contract drains wallet upon approval.
Fake trading platform. Allows small withdrawals initially, then blocks all withdrawals above threshold.
$25M Ponzi targeting Nigerian and Cameroonian immigrant communities in Maryland. 585+ investors. Promised 6% monthly. No actual investments made. WhatsApp-based recruitment.
Fake financial platform offering crypto, forex, stocks, commodities. False regulatory claims. Copied registration details. Virtual office addresses. Same template reused across multiple scams.
Promises daily profits with no trading mechanism. Pure Ponzi operation.
Fake Raydium airdrop on Solana. Claims free token distribution. Drains SOL and SPL tokens upon wallet connection. Targets Solana ecosystem users.
Selling land plots that either do not exist or have been multiply sold to different victims.
Secondary scam targeting previous fraud victims. Promises fund recovery for upfront fee.
Unlicensed binary options broker. Customer funds unrecoverable on closure.
Fictitious company SatCommTech promoted by AI Wealth/Lane Wealth. Fake SCT crypto security token offering. Company did not exist. Counterfeit whitepapers distributed.
FCA-listed fake bank. Email info@securepaybank.ltd. FCA specifically noted this is NOT connected to legitimate Securepaybank.com. Unlicensed deposit-taking operation.
VIP signal service claiming 95% accuracy. No auditable trade history provided.
Collapsed Ponzi that defrauded over 2,000 Nigerians. EFCC investigation ongoing.
Fake Solana airdrop claims wallet connection to drain funds. Classic approve-and-drain attack.
Vehicle auction scam advertising motorhomes/RVs. Victims wired payments for vehicles never delivered. Same infrastructure reused for crypto scams in 2024-2025.
Fake SushiSwap airdrop claim page. Claims SUSHI token redistribution. Drains tokens upon wallet connection. Uses .org for false legitimacy.
Fake Telegram Premium offer. Claims free premium subscription. Harvests phone numbers and payment details. 5th most-phished brand globally.
Impersonates Tesla/Elon Musk investment program. Promises guaranteed returns from 'Tesla automated trading system.' Classic celebrity impersonation investment fraud.
TikTok-branded fake investment scheme. Designed to target younger demographics.
Charges NGN 50,000 for VIP signals. Claims 98% accuracy with no verifiable record.
Fake P2P crypto exchange. Holds deposited funds then disappears with no support response.
Fake Trust Wallet synchronization portal. Claims wallet needs to be synced. Sole purpose is harvesting recovery phrases and private keys.
Fake UK scholarship fund. Charges GBP 200 application and IELTS exemption processing fee.
Fake Uniswap UNI token claim. Connects wallet and drains all tokens via malicious approval.
Promises to double USDT sent within hours. Classic flip Ponzi using Tether as vehicle.
Impersonates USPS. Most-phished brand 2024-2025 with 67,707+ phishing sites. Claims delivery failed refund. Harvests payment and personal information.
No verifiable registration with any securities regulator. 'Verified' claim is entirely self-declared.
Viral social media money-flip operation. Pays early senders to generate testimonials.
Linked to 8-year fraud network using same Iceland phone (+354-42-12434) and Reykjavik address. Previously ran vehicle auction scams. Faceless operation.
Charges upfront fees to recover lost crypto. Harvests any wallet credentials shared during 'recovery'.
Phishing site impersonating Wells Fargo. Fake account alerts to harvest login credentials and SSN. High-confidence confirmed phishing.
Sells fake Bitcoin mining hardware. Products never delivered after payment. Common scam targeting aspiring crypto miners in Nigeria and Africa.
Phishing site. Zenith Bank officially warned customers this domain is not affiliated with them.
Phishing clone targeting Zoom users. Fake security billing alert. Harvests corporate credentials and payment information.
